chunk_summary: This section outlines quality metrics and monthly audit cycles for tele-nutrition services, emphasizing safe longitudinal care, structured documentation, process reliability, and condition-specific outcome proxies within a digital workflow framework.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of structure, process, and outcome metrics in tele-nutrition quality measurement.
Learn how to implement monthly audit cycles to ensure safety and equity in tele-nutrition services.
Identify condition-specific outcome proxies for growth faltering, undernutrition, and obesity management.
Explore strategies for low-tech pathways and anthropometry verification in tele-nutrition.
key_facts: Structure metrics include mandatory fields like consent documentation, guardian verification, and red flag screening.
Process metrics track follow-up completion rates, overdue high-risk children contacted within a defined window, and time-to-escalation for red flags.
Outcome proxies include improved growth trajectory at 4 weeks, early weight gain or MUAC improvement at 2 weeks, and stabilization of BAZ trajectory for obesity.
Safety audits should verify anthropometry techniques via video/photo evidence and track low-tech pathway success rates.
Avoid reliance on satisfaction scores, as they may incentivize reassurance over necessary escalation.
reference_quote: Monitoring and evaluating digital health interventions (WHO, 2016).
